mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20d returns occur in more than 65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth yields positive 5d returns in more than 60% of observations during tightening regimes, or predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while the consumer price index remains above 220, the Federal Reserve restrictive stance dominates market pricing, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20d returns in more than 65% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven revenue growth for S&P 500 companies, producing positive forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 60d returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20d returns in more than 60% of quarters following negative GDP prints, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates inventory buildup without end-demand absorption, leading to near-term earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 5d returns in more than 65% of instances, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects.
